mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% triggers a labor market signal that indicates reduced consumer spending and increased risk aversion, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 6.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 100.5 signals a weakening demand cycle that reduces business investment and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 100.5 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 900,000 signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and increases risk aversion, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 900,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate and a rise in the CPI above 235 trigger a monetary and inflation signal that indicates reduced risk aversion and increased consumption, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in payroll employment above 138,000 signals an increase in labor market strength that reduces unemployment and increases consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 138,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP below 15900 signals a weakening demand cycle that reduces business investment and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 15900 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real sales above 427,000 signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and increases risk aversion, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 427,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
